description: ST category male, hill/border area (Uttarkashi, Uttarakhand), service sector — adventure tourism service; tests isHillBorderArea=true triggering special-category subsidy (35% for rural) for a non-women, non-OBC/SC applicant, with ST category as a secondary special-category trigger

business.name: Himalayan Adventure Trek & Tour Services
business.description: Adventure tourism service — trekking, camping, and guide services in hill area

notes: Hand-verified against PMEGP scheme rules + src/engines/financial-engine/index.ts and src/engines/eligibility-engine/index.ts (engine-verified by running computeFinancials + checkEligibility). Subsidy rate 35% — isSpecialCategory() (engine line 79-86) returns true via THREE independent triggers: applicant.category === 'ST' (in SPECIAL_CATEGORIES set), AND location.isHillBorderArea === true. Either alone is sufficient. Per subsidy_matrix.json SPECIAL+RURAL entry (line 28-35): subsidyRate 35, ownContributionPercent 5, maxProjectCost 5000000. Per pmegp_location_data.json (line 24): Uttarakhand is in hillBorderStates, with districts Uttarkashi/Chamoli/Rudraprayag/Pithoragarh/Bageshwar/Champawat listed — Uttarkashi qualifies, so isHillBorderArea=true is correct for this district. Note: task brief suggested 'special category + hill area' as additive, but the engine treats them as alternative triggers (no stacking — subsidy rate is 35% once special, not 35%+something for hill+ST). Location area=RURAL is what determines 35% vs 25% (urban) within the special branch; hill/border alone does not change the rate. Means-of-finance algebra: bankFinance = round((1500000 - 75000) / 1.35) = round(1425000 / 1.35) = 1055556 (rounded up from 1055555.555...); subsidyAmount = 1500000 - 75000 - 1055556 = 369444. bankTermLoan = bankFinance - workingCapital = 1055556 - 500000 = 555556. EMI (P=555556, r=0.11/12, n=84) = 9512. Break-even %: depreciation = 400000/10 = 40000; fixedCosts = 29000*12 + 40000 = 388000; variableCosts = 25000*12 = 300000; contribution = 150000*12 - 300000 = 1500000; breakEvenPercent = 388000/1500000*100 = 25.87%. DSCR: monthlyOperating = 82000+29000+25000 = 136000; annualRev = 1800000; annualExpenditure = 136000*12 + 40000 = 1672000; annualNetProfit = 128000; first-12-month debt service ≈ 12 * 9512 = 114144; DSCR = (128000 + 40000)/114144 = 1.47. Eligibility: all 7 engine checks pass (age 30 in [18,65], negative-list empty, cost 15L <= 25L SERVICE, no prior subsidy, INDIVIDUAL permitted, education 10TH_PASS rank 3 >= 8TH_PASS rank 2 required for cost > 10L — passes). No engine check exists for hill/border area — that flag only influences subsidy via isSpecialCategory(). breakEvenPercent and dscr rounded to 2 decimals; engine emits 25.866666666666667 and 1.471825063078217 — strict regression test should round to 2 decimals before comparing.
